methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The calculated LP token supply (D) must strictly satisfy the StableSwap invariant equation for the given reserves.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the iterative calculation of D diverges or loses precision, an attacker could mint unbacked LP tokens or withdraw more underlying assets than entitled, leading to pool insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_stableSwapMath(uint256[] memory reserves) public view { uint256 D = calcLpTokenSupply(reserves, data); uint256 sum = reserves[0] + reserves[1]; uint256 prod = reserves[0] * reserves[1]; assert(approxEq(4 * A * sum + D, 4 * A * D + (D**3) / (4 * prod))); }

invariants.description: The reserve calculation must be perfectly reversible with the LP token supply calculation within a precision of 1.
invariants.attack_scenario: If calcReserve and calcLpTokenSupply are not symmetric, the pricing curve is broken. Arbitrageurs can exploit the mathematical asymmetry to drain value from the pool through cyclic swaps.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_reserveReversibility(uint256 r0, uint256 r1) public view { uint256[] memory reserves = new uint256[](2); reserves[0] = r0; reserves[1] = r1; uint256 D = calcLpTokenSupply(reserves, data); uint256 calculatedR0 = calcReserve(reserves, 0, D, data); assert(calculatedR0 >= r0 - 1 && calculatedR0 <= r0 + 1); }

invariants.description: The reserve returned by calcReserveAtRatioSwap and calcReserveAtRatioLiquidity must yield a spot price within PRICE_THRESHOLD of the target price.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the Newton's method loop exhausts its 255 iterations without converging, the functions implicitly return 0 (default value of the named return variable). This causes a massive mispricing, allowing attackers to manipulate the oracle or swap at zero cost.
invariants.invariant_logic: let r = calcReserveAtRatioSwap(reserves, j, ratios); let actualPrice = calcRate(newReserves); abs(actualPrice - targetPrice) <= PRICE_THRESHOLD
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_priceConvergence(uint256[] memory reserves, uint256 j, uint256[] memory ratios) public view { uint256 targetPrice = (ratios[i] * 1e6) / ratios[j]; uint256 outReserve = calcReserveAtRatioSwap(reserves, j, ratios, data); uint256[] memory newReserves = new uint256[](2); newReserves[0] = reserves[0]; newReserves[1] = reserves[1]; newReserves[j] = outReserve; uint256 actualPrice = calcRate(newReserves, i, j, data); assert(actualPrice >= targetPrice - PRICE_THRESHOLD && actualPrice <= targetPrice + PRICE_THRESHOLD); }

invariants.description: The LP token supply must be monotonically increasing with respect to the pool reserves.
invariants.attack_scenario: If adding liquidity decreases the total LP supply due to overflow or precision loss in the iterative calculation, LPs would lose funds upon depositing, and attackers could manipulate the pool's invariant.
invariants.invariant_logic: calcLpTokenSupply([R0 + delta0, R1 + delta1]) >= calcLpTokenSupply([R0, R1])
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_lpSupplyMonotonicity(uint256 r0, uint256 r1, uint256 d0, uint256 d1) public view { uint256[] memory res1 = new uint256[](2); res1[0] = r0; res1[1] = r1; uint256[] memory res2 = new uint256[](2); res2[0] = r0 + d0; res2[1] = r1 + d1; uint256 supply1 = calcLpTokenSupply(res1, data); uint256 supply2 = calcLpTokenSupply(res2, data); assert(supply2 >= supply1); }

invariants.description: A Well must strictly contain unique tokens; duplicate tokens must be rejected during initialization.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a pool is initialized with the same token for both reserves, the StableSwap invariant math breaks down. An attacker could exploit the self-referential reserves to artificially inflate LP token supply and drain the protocol.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_uniqueTokens() public view { IERC20[] memory t = tokens(); assert(t[0] != t[1]); }
